title: Add JJF_ACTOR env / --actor flag for multi-agent claim attribution
body:
# Goal

Add the orchestrator-supplied actor override chain that
multi-agent workflows need. Today `jjf update <id> --claim`
(and `jjf comment <id>` for author) resolves the current
identity from `jj config get user.name` only. That's fine
for a single-human / single-machine setup, but a fan-out
of N agents from one machine all claim as the same person
— useless for telling agents apart in the `assignee` field
afterward.

Mirror beads' precedence chain at
`reference/beads/cmd/bd/main.go:459-492`:

```
--actor <name> flag > JJF_ACTOR env > jj config get user.name > error
```

`jjf comment` should follow the same chain for its author
field (today the chain is `--author` flag > jj config
user.name/user.email — extend the middle).

# Why now

The `host-asterinas-migrate` (`cc2fa96`) fan-out is the
concrete driver. ~30 Haiku agents per the audit doc, all
forked from one orchestrator machine. With today's
behavior they all claim as the same name; the assignee
column can't tell them apart, and a post-migration audit
("which agent imported phase-2 epic-04?") becomes
impossible.

# Spec / API

## Resolution chain

`resolve_current_user()` (at `crates/jjf/src/main.rs:3367-3380`)
becomes:

1. The CLI's `--actor <name>` flag if present and non-empty.
2. `JJF_ACTOR` env var if present and non-empty.
3. `jj config get user.name`.
4. `CliError::NoCurrentUser` if all are empty.

Validation: same as today — single-line, trimmed, non-empty.
The newline-reject path already exists in storage's
`set_assignee` validator at `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs`
(grep for `validate_no_newlines`); the resolve step just
needs to return a clean string.

## `--actor` flag

Add `--actor <name>` to `Commands::Update` and
`Commands::Comment`. Optional; when present, takes
precedence over env and config. Helps the
orchestrator that wants to override per-invocation
without exporting env state. Document as "for
multi-agent orchestrators; everyone else should set
jj user.name."

Mutually exclusive with `--unset-assignee` and
`--unclaim` (they don't take an identity).

## Comment author

Extend `resolve_author()` (at
`crates/jjf/src/main.rs:3399ish`) similarly:

```
--author flag > JJF_ACTOR env > jj config user.name + user.email > error
```

The `--author` override stays as-is (it accepts a full
`Name <email>` string verbatim). The new middle slot
honors `JJF_ACTOR` and synthesizes
`$JJF_ACTOR <user.email-fallback>` so the resulting
comment-author string is canonical-shape.

# Out of scope

- Identity verification / public-key auth. Anyone setting
  `JJF_ACTOR=alice` "is" alice; jjforge isn't an
  authentication boundary.
- Persistent agent registration. The env-var-per-process
  model is the contract.
- Per-actor permissions, ACLs, role assignment. Tracker
  semantics, not a security model.
- Renaming existing assignees in the repo. Hygiene pass
  for later.

# Acceptance

- [ ] `JJF_ACTOR=alice ./jjf update <id> --claim` writes
      `assignee: alice` regardless of `jj user.name`.
- [ ] `./jjf update <id> --claim --actor bob` writes
      `assignee: bob`.
- [ ] With neither override set, behavior matches today
      (read `jj user.name`).
- [ ] `--actor ""` and `JJF_ACTOR=""` (empty string) fall
      through to the next slot rather than writing empty
      assignee — empty is "not set."
- [ ] `--actor` conflicts cleanly with `--unset-assignee`
      and `--unclaim` (clap conflict-with).
- [ ] `jjf comment <id> -F -` honors the same chain for
      its `author` field (extended `resolve_author`).
- [ ] Unit tests cover each slot of the chain (env-only,
      flag-only, flag-over-env, config-fallback, empty-
      chain → `NoCurrentUser`).
- [ ] `docs/cli-json.md` notes the new flag.
- [ ] CLAUDE.md gets a one-line addition under the
      "Orchestrating work" section noting the env-var
      convention for fan-out dispatches.

# Cross-refs

- Beads' resolution chain:
  `reference/beads/cmd/bd/main.go:459-492`.
- Beads' multi-agent convention is documented in
  `prime.go` and the `--claim` CAS at
  `reference/beads/internal/storage/domain/db/issue.go:185-214`.
- jjforge's current resolver:
  `crates/jjf/src/main.rs:3367-3380` (claim) and
  `:3399ish` (comment author).
- The `host-asterinas-migrate` fan-out (`cc2fa96`) is
  the concrete consumer.
